text.title: JUnit 5 + Spring Boot Test Failure Modes
text.summary: Four Spring Boot test antipatterns. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Every test class uses `@SpringBootTest`. Test suite takes 20 minutes; developers stop running it locally; CI flakes.
antipattern.root-cause: Slice-appropriate annotations skipped; full context boot per test.
antipattern.fix: Replace controller tests with `@WebMvcTest`, services with `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`, repositories with `@DataJpaTest`. Reserve `@SpringBootTest` for genuine end-to-end integration.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass on H2; production fails on Postgres with `data exception: invalid input syntax for jsonb`. Repository code relied on Postgres JSONB.
antipattern.root-cause: In-memory H2 used for relational integration test.
antipattern.fix: Switch to Testcontainers Postgres for any test that depends on production dialect. Keep H2 only for stateless unit-of-test scenarios where SQL is trivial.

antipattern.symptom: `new OrderService(new MockRepository())` in tests. Each test wires collaborators manually; mock state leaks between tests; tests don't verify the real DI graph.
antipattern.fix: Add `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`. Declare `@Mock` for collaborators, `@InjectMocks` for the unit under test. Mockito resets mocks between tests.

antipattern.symptom: Reactive/async test method declared `void`; assertion failures pass silently. CI green, prod broken.
antipattern.root-cause: Async test must return `Mono`, `Flux`, `CompletableFuture`, or use `await` helpers; not `void`.
antipattern.fix: Return appropriate async type. Use `StepVerifier` for Reactor; `Awaitility` for general async. Never `void` for async work.
